name: SignNow API
what_it_does: E-signature platform API for sending documents for signature, managing templates, tracking signing status, and collecting legally binding electronic signatures at lower cost than DocuSign.
use_cases: Sending agreements and contracts for e-signature from automated workflows
Creating document templates for repeatable signing workflows
Tracking document completion status from agent pipelines
Bulk document sending with field pre-fill from database data
Healthcare consent form collection with HIPAA compliance
not_for: Complex enterprise signature workflows requiring Salesforce/SAP integrations
Legal-grade advanced electronic signatures in the EU (eIDAS qualified signatures)
Very high-volume document processing requiring enterprise SLA
best_when: You need a cost-effective e-signature API for sending contracts and collecting signatures, especially in healthcare (HIPAA) or where DocuSign pricing is prohibitive.
avoid_when: You need deep enterprise CRM integrations, advanced routing logic, or LTV (long-term validation) signatures.

auth.notes: OAuth2 with bearer tokens. Basic auth available for token generation. Access tokens expire in 24 hours. Refresh tokens available. API credentials tied to your SignNow account.

pricing.notes: API access requires Business plan or higher. Lower cost than DocuSign for similar functionality. HIPAA BAA available on Business Premium and Enterprise.

performance.rate_limits: Not publicly documented; enterprise accounts have higher limits

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: JSON error responses with message field. Error codes not always documented. Some error messages are terse and require trial-and-error to diagnose.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No idempotency keys. Document sending is not idempotent. Agents must track sent document IDs.
known_agent_gotchas: Document field coordinates (position of signature fields) must be specified in precise pixel positions
Access tokens expire in 24 hours — must implement token refresh for long-running agents
Template creation from existing document requires multi-step process
Webhook configuration requires specifying exact event types — not all events enabled by default
API documentation is less comprehensive than DocuSign or PandaDoc — expect some trial and error
HIPAA compliance requires explicit Business Agreement and specific plan tier
